name: Feature / enhancement
about: New work, refactor, or design proposal. Bug reports use the Bug template.
title: ""
labels: enhancement

## Status (YYYY-MM-DD)

<!--
Mandatory at filing time. Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date.

Use this block as the living truth: when state changes (work starts,
acceptance bullets ship, scope narrows, deps close), edit THIS block
with a new date line and what changed. Leave the rest of the body as
the original framing.

If the issue is brand-new and no work has started:
> Not started. Filed YYYY-MM-DD.

If partial progress: state which bullets shipped (with PR refs) and
which remain. Then list the remaining acceptance below.

Convention from the 2026-05-17 issue-tracker audit: bodies without a
dated status block age into fiction within a month. Bodies with one
stay useful for the duration of the issue.
-->

Not started. Filed YYYY-MM-DD.

## Goal

What problem does this solve? One paragraph.

## Acceptance

<!-- Concrete, checkable bullets. "Compiles" is not acceptance.
"Test X passes against state Y" is. -->

- [ ]
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Out of scope

What this issue intentionally does not cover. List the adjacent work
that might tempt scope creep and explain why it's a separate concern.

## Related

<!-- Cross-references to other issues. Use #NNN syntax. When citing a
file path, that path must EXIST at filing time (`scripts/check-issue-body-staleness.sh`
catches phantom paths). Avoid line numbers in issue bodies; they
drift on every PR. Use symbol names instead (`searchSymbols` function
in the Search package, not `Search/Foo.swift:142`). -->
